LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE A LACK OF EVIDENCE OF KNOWLEDGE OF THE POTENTIAL ASSAULT BEFORE IT OCCURRED AND AS A CONSEQUENCE, DOES NOT NEGATE THE CLAIM OF DUTY. ON THE ISSUE OF BREACH OF DUTY, THE HUI DECLARATION IN THE MOVING PAPERS IS AMBIGUOUS AS TO WHEN THE CALL WAS MADE TO CENTRAL CONTROL. THE BROWNE DECLARATION ALSO CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. ON THE ISSUE OF CAUSATION, THE BROWNE DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE BROWNE DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE TO ADDRESS A COMMON CARRIER'S DUTY. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement Of Deft Ccsf;
Off Calendar Apr-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LUCIA CHEN-LUFTIG, ROCHELLE REYES, and, CALIFORNIA EMERGENCY PHYSICIANS MEDICAL GROUP'S Motion to Contest the Good Faith Settlement of Defendant CCSF. Appearances: Gary A. Angel, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 788-5935, appearing for Plaintiff; and opposing party; Mary M. Sackett, Esq. of The Goldman Law Firm (415) 435-5500, appearing for Defendants and moving party; Robert Bonta, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4268, appearing for CCSF, and opposing party. Argued and the Court vacated its tentative ruling, and rules as follows: Motion is denied, and Court affirms the Good Faith Settlement between the Defendant City and County of San Francisco, and Plaintiff Timothy J Jacques. Order Determining Good Faith Settlement between Defendant City and County of San Francisco and Plaintiff Timothy J. Jacques filed in Open Court. (302CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Frear Stephen Schmid, Esq. 788-5957 for pltf & opposing party; Robert A. Bonta, Deputy City Atty 554-4268 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied for failure to shift the burden of producing evidence. Defendant has failed to demonstrate a lack of evidence of knowledge of the potential assault before it occurred and as a consequence, does not negate the claim of duty. On the issue of breach of duty, the Hui declaration in the moving papers is ambiguous as to when the call was made to central control. The Browne Declaration also creates a triable issue of fact. On the issue of causation, the Browne Declaration creates a triable issue of fact. The Browne Declaration is admissible to address a common carrier's duty. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence are overruled. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
